headlines.title: Kevin Warsh Is Confirmed as Fed Chair in 54-45 Senate Vote
Trump Can Keep Collecting Global 10% Tariffs After Appeals Court Stays Decision
Wholesale Inflation Shot Higher in April; PPI Surges 6.0% Annually
Trump-Xi Summit Dominates Global Headlines As Trade and AI Competition Take Focus
Oil Price Today: Crude oil snaps 3-day fall ahead of Trump's China visit

notes: S&P 500 and NASDAQ reached new record highs, driven by AI and semiconductor optimism despite hotter-than-expected April PPI inflation data.
The U.S. 10-year Treasury yield rose to 4.479% due to heightened inflation expectations.
Brent Crude prices slipped to $105.63 as markets weigh fragile Middle East ceasefire negotiations against an expected contraction in global oil demand.
Global markets remain highly sensitive to trade updates as a U.S. appeals court allowed the 10% global tariffs to proceed during the Trump-Xi summit in Beijing.
